attacking_style: Opportunistic breakaway rider who conserves energy by wheelsucking stronger riders, then exploits freshness in final sprint. Capitalizes on others' bridging efforts rather than forcing early moves.
team_tactics: Limited data, but shows ability to operate independently in breakaways without significant team support, relying on tactical positioning over domestique work.
terrain_preference: Breakaway-friendly stages where selective groups form; benefits from scenarios requiring bridging efforts that allow him to save energy while others work.
vulnerability: Relies heavily on cooperation of stronger riders to reach winning positions; may struggle when forced to bridge gaps or control breakaways alone without tactical allies.
key_patterns: Employs wheelsucking tactics to stay fresh for final sprint after others work (1 observation)
Wins from breakaways rather than bunch sprints, targeting reduced group scenarios (1 observation)
Capitalizes on others' bridging work with 20km remaining for positioning advantage (1 observation)
form_trajectory: unknown: Only single race observation available; Grand Tour win demonstrates capability but insufficient data for trajectory assessment
